test.name: FamilySearch wiki request should route to search-familysearch-wiki
test.description: User asks for the FamilySearch Research Wiki, not Wikipedia. The two are different sources — Wikipedia is the general encyclopedia (search-wikipedia), FamilySearch Wiki is the FamilySearch-curated genealogy reference (search-familysearch-wiki). The skill must not blur this distinction.

input.user_message: Search the FamilySearch wiki for guidance on how to find Italian birth records

negative.explanation: 'FamilySearch wiki' is the user's explicit naming of a different source — the FamilySearch Research Wiki, which is searched via the search-familysearch-wiki MCP-backed skill, not the general Wikipedia that search-wikipedia targets. Calling search-wikipedia here would return a Wikipedia article about Italian birth records or about FamilySearch itself, neither of which is what the user asked for.

judge_context: Should explicitly route to search-familysearch-wiki rather than calling wikipedia_search on a related query
